entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The legume crop being studied for heat tolerance.

entities.name: HEAT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of the plant to maintain productivity under high temperature conditions.

entities.name: HIGH SOIL TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Elevated soil temperature, specifically 38 °C in the study.

entities.name: HIGH AIR TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Elevated air temperature, specifically 38 °C and 40 °C in the study.

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE PHASE
entities.description: The stage of plant development including podding, microsporogenesis, and flowering.

entities.name: ARID AND SEMI-ARID CROPPING ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Environments characterized by low rainfall and high temperatures.

entities.name: CROP GROWTH RATE
entities.description: The rate of overall crop biomass increase, denoted as CGR.

entities.name: POD GROWTH RATE
entities.description: The rate of pod development, denoted as PGR.

entities.name: PARTITIONING
entities.description: The ratio of pod growth rate to crop growth rate (PGR:CGR).

entities.name: FRUIT-SET
entities.description: The successful development of fruit after pollination.

entities.name: MICROSPOROGENESIS
entities.description: The process of pollen grain formation occurring before flowering.

entities.name: FLOWERING
entities.description: The stage of plant development where flowers are produced.

entities.name: CELLULAR MEMBRANE THERMOSTABILITY
entities.description: The ability of cell membranes to remain stable under heat stress.

entities.name: RELATIVE INJURY
entities.description: A measurement used to assess cellular membrane thermostability.

entities.name: SCREENING
entities.description: The process of evaluating multiple genotypes to identify those with specific traits.

entities.name: POD WEIGHT
entities.description: The mass of the pods produced by the plant.
